title: If Shiro Sagisu Hadnt Done the Music
display_title: If Shiro Sagisu Hadnt Done the Music
wikitext:
[]: An odd musical thought             Quote Post by Dave » Tue Oct 19, 2004 8:47 pm I wonder, how would Evangelion have turned out if Yuki Kajiura had done most of it's music? Her work on .hack//sign and Noir was astounding, and it seems she really has a knack for mystery's. And Evangelion sure seems like one hell of a mystery to me. =3 Perhaps we could convince (threaten) her to particpate in the live action project? Hah! I wish...

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Wed Oct 20, 2004 9:29 am :? hmmmmmm... Yuki Kajiura you say? It would definetely be interesting to hear. Maybe Eva would move away from the the somewhat classical feel of its BGM to a different style. Would that be good? Bad? I'd say good, just to hear it out

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 20, 2004 11:09 am Yuki Kajiura is a woman, just so you know.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Oct 20, 2004 11:11 am In general, I found a lot of Eva's music to be kind of dull. It worked, but on its own, its not really listenable. I think truly good music composition can stand on its own and be enjoyable. .hack's music was very fitting for the show, and most of it is excellent, even without the animation. I wish Eva had that kind of music the first time around.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Wed Oct 20, 2004 11:16 am Reichu wrote: Yuki Kajiura is a woman, just so you know.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Wed Oct 20, 2004 7:38 pm No prob Dave, To be honest, I assumed she was a guy as well Oh well, no biggie. I've made a few Eva OSTs ('cause I'm too cheap to buy them :roll: ) and after listening to all the BGM on its own, I agree. However, certain pieces, such as Rei II, Berserk, The hardy feeling of freedom, CA'sT instrumental and others were big hits as stand alone music in my opinion. :roll: Once again my spelling sucks.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Wed Oct 20, 2004 7:49 pm I've never heard any of Yuki's music, unfortunately, but I agree that some of Shirou Sagisu's music isn't entirely well structured. Although I think he's really good (really really good), he's one who is better at making themes. By the way, I really enjoy Both of You, Dance like You Want to Win. Maybe they could have that again except redone by the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra! 8) I heard some glitches in the original recording. I guess they weren't well rehearsed.

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Thu Oct 21, 2004 2:22 pm MAGI wrote: By the way, I really enjoy Both of You, Dance like You Want to Win. Maybe they could have that again except redone by the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra! 8)

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Oct 21, 2004 5:44 pm After I heard Eva Symphony, I thought that Sagisu's score was extremely reminiscent of classic John Williams, perhaps a little too reminiscent. In replying, I forgot what the topic of this thread was, so I'll just make the comment that my favorite piece from Eva is Borderline Case. That's got "the calibur of recognition equal to the 'doo-dee-doo-doo-doop' of Close Encounters" written all over it. Oh, and plus it sounds good.

[]:             Quote Post by The Lunatic » Thu Nov 25, 2004 12:25 am Personally, I'd find it interesting if Anno turned End of EVA into a musical using tracks from Pink Floyd's The Wall ... :idea:

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Nov 25, 2004 12:43 am That explains the name...

[]:             Quote Post by The Lunatic » Thu Nov 25, 2004 12:48 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Fri Nov 26, 2004 11:38 am Has anyone ever seen this one AMV of Cowboy Bebop's theme song done up with Evangelion characters? It's really, really awesome. I saw it at a convention, and everyone in the room clapped after it was done. Which brings up a question... how do you think the mood of Evangelion might have changed if someone like Yoko Kanno composed the soundtrack? Or maybe even Nobuo Uematsu (Final Fantasy)?

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Fri Nov 26, 2004 8:18 pm We may never know until we ask them to have their take on the music, themselves, then 'dub' them in... which wouldn't be a bad idea, and would be fun. One thing we know for sure is... it will not be the same... without Shirou Sagisu. :cry: But like I say, it should be fun. :P Maybe, if I could make my own music, I'll try it myself.

[]:             Quote Post by Dave » Sat Nov 27, 2004 2:26 am Ayanami wrote: Has anyone ever seen this one AMV of Cowboy Bebop's theme song done up with Evangelion characters? It's really, really awesome. I saw it at a convention, and everyone in the room clapped after it was done. Which brings up a question... how do you think the mood of Evangelion might have changed if someone like Yoko Kanno composed the soundtrack? Or maybe even Nobuo Uematsu (Final Fantasy)?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Nov 29, 2004 2:38 pm I think that One Winged Angel would be good for Eva that song is badass

[]: If Shiro Sagisu Hadn't Done the Music...             Quote Post by Evayo » Mon May 25, 2009 7:43 pm So my dad and I were watching Eva the other day. Being a big music buff, he kept complimenting Shiro Sagisu's score, especially during the calm, quiet scenes and the mind trips. We started discussing who we would have picked to do the music score for NGE if Sagisu hadn't been hired to do it. My question is: If Shiro Sagisu hadn't done the music for Evangelion, who would you have picked, Japanese or otherwise? I would have picked either Yuki Kajiura or John Powell. Yuki, because her music is always breathtakingly gorgeous and somewhat haunting, and Powell because he can deliver some powerful suspense/battle themes.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Mon May 25, 2009 7:55 pm Yusuke Honma <333 just because he did the music for yu yu hakusho and flame of recca and that music makes me all happy inside

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon May 25, 2009 8:29 pm If someone else did it i'd probably not enjoy the soundtrack as much as i do

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon May 25, 2009 8:30 pm Axell wrote: If someone else did it i'd probably not enjoy the soundtrack as much as I do

Article: If Shiro Sagisu Hadnt Done the Music
25, 2009 7:43 pm So my dad and I were watching Eva the other day. Being a big music buff, he kept complimenting Shiro Sagisu's score, especially during the calm, quiet scenes and the mind trips. We started discussing who we would have picked to do the music score for NGE if Sagisu hadn't been hired to do it. My question is: If Shiro Sagisu hadn't done the music for Evangelion, who would you have picked, Japanese or otherwise? I would have picked either Yuki Kajiura or John Powell. Yuki, because her music is always breathtakingly gorgeous and somewhat haunting, and Powell because he can deliver some powerful suspense/battle themes.

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Mon May 25, 2009 7:55 pm Yusuke Honma <333 just because he did the music for yu yu hakusho and flame of recca and that music makes me all happy inside
